title: Rose body butter
subtitle: A whipped shea and cocoa butter blend with rose geranium
excerpt: A whipped body butter made from shea butter, cocoa butter, and jojoba oil, scented with rose geranium essential oil. Whipping incorporates air to make the texture light and fluffy rather than solid. Anhydrous and preservative-free; keeps 6-8 months.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry body butter formulation notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026), shea and cocoa butter ratios for whipped consistency. Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), p. 389, rose geranium dermal limits.

recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly. Use a stand mixer for larger batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use once whipped and set. Shelf life 6-8 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6-8 months in a cool, dry location. Anhydrous; no preservative needed. Whipped body butters melt above 30°C and lose their fluffy texture; store below room temperature in summer. Discard if the butter smells rancid or paint-like.

glossaryTerms.definition: Contains no water. Anhydrous cosmetics do not need a preservative.

content.text: A whipped rose body butter. Shea butter at 60% provides the base; cocoa butter at 20% adds hardness at room temperature; jojoba oil at 20% thins the blend slightly and gives a less greasy finish. Melting the blend, chilling until partially set, then whipping incorporates air and transforms the texture from solid to fluffy. Rose geranium essential oil at 1% provides the fragrance.
and needs no preservative. However, if water from wet hands is introduced into the container the shelf life drops dramatically; always use dry hands or a spatula.

items.prepNote: unrefined shea butter for maximum skin benefit, or refined for a neutral scent. 60% of the batch.
unrefined or refined cocoa butter. 20% of the batch. Provides hardness so the whipped butter holds its texture at room temperature.
jojoba oil. 20% of the batch. Technically a liquid wax rather than an oil; very stable with a long shelf life. Lightens the texture.
Pelargonium graveolens rose geranium essential oil. 1% of the batch (approximately 2 g ≈ 2 ml). Added after partial cooling, before whipping.

content.text: Melt the butters and oil together

Place the shea butter, cocoa butter, and jojoba oil in a heatproof bowl over a saucepan of barely simmering water. Stir gently as the butters melt; the cocoa butter is the hardest and takes the longest. When the cocoa butter has fully liquefied and the blend is clear, remove from the heat. The temperature will be around 65-70°C.

Place the bowl in the fridge for 30-45 minutes, or in the freezer for 15-20 minutes. Check every 10 minutes; the mixture is ready to whip when it is the consistency of soft butter, opaque and slightly firm around the edges but not solid throughout. If it sets completely, leave at room temperature for 10 minutes to soften slightly before whipping.

Using a hand mixer on medium speed, whip the partially-set butter blend for 3-5 minutes until light and fluffy. If using fragrance, add the rose geranium essential oil during the last 30 seconds of whipping. The finished texture should be soft, airy, and hold a peak. Spoon into a clean, dry jar and leave to set at room temperature for 2 hours.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the wrist and wait 24 hours before wider use. Rose geranium essential oil at 1% is well within the IFRA leave-on cosmetic limit, but individual sensitivities to Pelargonium vary.

6-8 months in a cool, dry location. The whipped texture softens and loses its airy consistency in a warm room above 25°C; store in a cool cupboard in summer. Always use dry hands or a clean spatula to scoop from the jar; water contamination dramatically shortens shelf life. Discard if the butter develops a sharp, rancid, or paint-like smell.
